name: Alabama
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from state income tax
All active duty pay: 100% exempt from Alabama income tax
Disabled vet property tax exemption: up to $99,400 assessed value (10%+ rating)
Free hunting and fishing license: 20%+ service-connected disabled veterans
Multiple free veteran specialty license plates available
Veterans cemetery: free burial for eligible veterans
State hiring preference: veterans receive absolute preference for state jobs
property_tax_notes: Tiered property tax exemptions based on disability rating. 100% disabled: full exemption on primary residence.
notes: Alabama consistently ranks among the top 10 most veteran-friendly states for tax treatment of military income.

name: Alaska
highlights: NO state income tax on any income (wages, retirement, military pay)
NO state sales tax (some local municipalities have sales tax)
Property tax exemption: up to $150,000 assessed value for disabled veterans
Military retirement pay: not subject to Alaska income tax (no state income tax)
Free hunting and fishing license: disabled veterans with 50%+ rating
Alaska Permanent Fund Dividend: all Alaska residents including military stationed there
Veterans cemetery: free burial at Fort Richardson National Cemetery
property_tax_notes: Significant property tax exemption for disabled vets. No state income tax makes Alaska one of the most financially favorable states.
notes: Alaska residents (including military stationed there for sufficient time) may qualify for the annual Permanent Fund Dividend (~$1,000–$3,000/year in most years).

name: Arizona
stars: Strong property exemptions for disabled vets
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Arizona state income tax
Active duty combat pay: 100% exempt from Arizona income tax
Disabled veteran property tax exemption on primary residence
100% disabled veteran: significant property tax exemption on home
Free hunting and fishing license: honorably discharged veterans
Vehicle registration fee waiver or reduction for disabled veterans
Veteran specialty license plates: multiple options
Strong veteran hiring preference in state government
property_tax_notes: Arizona has strong property tax relief programs for disabled veterans — verify current amounts with Arizona Department of Revenue.
notes: Arizona is a popular retirement destination for military retirees due to the 100% retirement pay exemption, warm climate, and strong VA healthcare network.

name: Arkansas
stars: Full property tax exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans
highlights: Military retirement pay: up to $6,000 exempt; phasing toward full exemption
Combat pay: exempt from Arkansas income tax
100% P&T disabled veteran: full property tax exemption on primary residence
Surviving spouse of 100% disabled veteran: maintains property tax exemption
Free vehicle license plates for veterans with qualifying ratings
Veteran hiring preference for state employment
State veterans cemeteries: Little Rock and Birdeye
property_tax_notes: 100% P&T disabled veterans receive full property tax exemption on their primary residence — a major benefit.
notes: Arkansas retirement pay exemption is growing — check current legislation for the most up-to-date phase-in amounts.

name: California
stars: Military pay exempt while stationed outside CA
highlights: Military pay: 100% exempt from California income tax while stationed outside CA
Nonresident military stationed in CA: only CA-source income taxed
Veterans exemption: up to $4,000 property value exempt (basic exemption)
Disabled veteran exemption: up to $196,262 property value exempt (100% disabled)
CalVet home loan program: competitive rates for veterans buying in CA
State hiring preference: 10-point preference for disabled veterans
College fee waiver: dependents of 100% disabled veterans at CA public colleges
Disabled veteran business enterprise (DVBE) program for business owners
property_tax_notes: Disabled Veteran Exemption covers up to $196,262 (basic) or $295,230+ (low-income threshold) in assessed home value for qualifying disabled veterans.
notes: California's high cost of living partially offset by strong disabled vet and dependent education benefits. Military retirement pay IS taxed in California — a significant factor for retirement location decisions.

name: Colorado
highlights: Military retirement pay: up to $24,000 exempt from Colorado income tax (age 55+; increasing annually toward full exemption)
Active duty pay: exempt while stationed outside Colorado
Disabled veteran property tax reduction: varies by county and disability rating
National Guard: full in-state tuition waiver at all Colorado public colleges
Veterans license plates: multiple options including Purple Heart
Veteran employment assistance: strong VETS programs
Colorado Veterans Trust Fund: emergency financial assistance
property_tax_notes: Property tax relief programs available; amounts vary by county. Verify with your county assessor.
notes: Colorado's retirement exemption is increasing incrementally toward full exemption — check current legislative status for the most recent amount.

name: Connecticut
stars: Military pay earned in CT: 100% exempt from state income tax
highlights: Active duty military pay earned in Connecticut: 100% exempt from state income tax
Military pension: partial income exemption (income-dependent)
Property tax exemption: $1,500–$5,000 assessed value reduction depending on service era and disability
Disabled veteran: additional property tax exemption
Free hunting and fishing license: active duty personnel deployed outside CT
Veterans wartime service medal for qualifying service periods
Veteran preference in state employment
property_tax_notes: Property tax exemptions vary by municipality — some towns offer additional local exemptions beyond the state minimum.
notes: Connecticut's exemption is primarily for active duty pay earned in-state. Retirement pay is partially taxable depending on income level.

name: Delaware
highlights: Military retirement pay: first $2,000 exempt from Delaware income tax
Combat pay: 100% exempt
Property tax credit: ~$400/year for qualifying veterans
Veteran employment preference for state jobs
Free veteran ID cards
Delaware Veterans Home: assisted living and skilled nursing care for qualifying veterans
State veterans cemetery: free burial for eligible Delaware veterans
property_tax_notes: Property tax credit of ~$400/year. Modest but consistent benefit.
notes: Delaware's benefits are modest by military-friendly state standards, but the state has no sales tax — a meaningful saving for daily purchases.

name: Florida
stars: Full homestead exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans
highlights: NO state income tax on any income (military pay, retirement, civilian wages)
100% P&T disabled veteran: full homestead property tax exemption on primary residence
Disabled veteran homestead exemption: tiered reductions for ratings below 100%
Surviving spouse of 100% disabled veteran: full exemption transfers to spouse
Free Florida state park access: disabled veterans and their family members
Free hunting and fishing license: 10%+ service-connected disabled veterans
Veteran hiring preference: absolute preference in state government hiring
Florida Veterans' Benefits Guide: comprehensive state program listing
property_tax_notes: The full property tax exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans can save $5,000–$20,000+/year on Florida property taxes depending on home value and county millage rate.
notes: Florida is consistently rated among the top 2–3 most veteran-friendly states for tax purposes. No income tax plus full property exemption for 100% P&T is an extremely powerful combination.

name: Georgia
stars: Up to $35,000 retirement pay exempt per retiree
highlights: Military retirement pay: up to $35,000 exempt from Georgia income tax per person (over 62)
Active duty pay: exempt from Georgia income tax while on active duty
Disabled veteran homestead exemption: varies by disability rating
National Guard and Reserve: state tuition waiver program
Free hunting and fishing license: 100% disabled veterans
Veteran preference: strong state employment preference
Georgia Veterans Service: VSO network for claims assistance
property_tax_notes: Homestead exemptions for disabled veterans available — amounts vary by county and disability rating.
notes: Georgia's retirement pay exemption is age-gated (62+). Active duty pay exemption is a strong benefit for those currently serving.

name: Hawaii
stars: Military pay earned OCONUS: exempt from Hawaii income tax
highlights: Military pay: 100% exempt from Hawaii income tax while stationed or serving outside Hawaii
Nonresident military stationed in Hawaii: exempted from Hawaii income tax on military pay
Veteran property tax exemptions: vary by county (Honolulu, Maui, Hawaii, Kauai counties have different amounts)
Free Hawaii state park admission
Hawaii Veteran Loan Program (HiVA): home loans for veterans
Disabled veteran property tax exemption: enhanced amounts for 100% disabled
Strong TRICARE supplemental care network at Tripler Army Medical Center
property_tax_notes: Property tax exemptions vary by county — Honolulu County's basic veteran exemption is $75,000 assessed value; 100% disabled may qualify for full exemption.
notes: Hawaii's high cost of living makes the pay exemption for OCONUS-earned income particularly valuable for dual-income military families. Retirement pay IS taxable in Hawaii — a significant retirement location consideration.

name: Idaho
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Idaho state income tax
Active duty pay: exempt while serving outside Idaho
Property tax reduction: disabled veterans qualifying for circuit breaker exemption
Free hunting and fishing license: 40%+ service-connected disabled veterans
Vehicle registration: reduced fees and veteran plate options
POW/MIA: free lifetime hunting and fishing licenses
Veteran preference: strong state hiring preference
property_tax_notes: Property tax circuit breaker program provides meaningful relief for qualifying veterans — verify current income and disability thresholds with Idaho State Tax Commission.
notes: Idaho's full retirement pay exemption combined with lower property values (vs. coasts) makes it a strong financial choice for retirees.

name: Illinois
stars: Illinois Veterans Grant: free in-state tuition
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Illinois state income tax
Active duty pay: 100% exempt from Illinois income tax
Property tax homestead exemption for veterans with disabilities
Illinois Veterans Grant (IVG): free tuition at Illinois public colleges and universities for eligible veterans
Free Illinois state park admission
Combat Bonus Program: bonus payments for combat veterans
Strong Illinois Department of Veterans Affairs VSO network
property_tax_notes: Veterans with service-connected disabilities may qualify for property tax homestead exemption. Amount varies with disability rating.
notes: The Illinois Veterans Grant (free tuition at public colleges) is one of the strongest education benefits offered by any state — an $8,000–$15,000/year value for qualifying veterans.

name: Indiana
stars: $12,480 property tax deduction for disabled veterans
highlights: Military retirement pay: deduction up to $6,250 from Indiana adjusted gross income
Combat pay: fully exempt from Indiana income tax
Property tax deduction: $12,480 for veterans with qualifying disability
Surviving spouse: maintains property tax deduction
Free hunting and fishing license: veterans with qualifying disability ratings
Veteran employment preference
Indiana Veterans Home: care for qualifying veterans
property_tax_notes: $12,480 property tax deduction (from assessed value) for veterans with service-connected disabilities. Modest but consistent.
notes: Indiana's benefits are moderate. The combat pay exemption is a meaningful benefit for those currently deployed.

name: Iowa
stars: 100% military retirement pay tax exempt (as of 2024)
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Iowa income tax (as of 2024 full phase-in)
Active duty pay: 100% exempt from Iowa income tax
Military Homestead Tax Credit: provides property tax credit for veterans
Veterans Trust Fund: emergency financial assistance for Iowa veterans in need
Free hunting and fishing license: 70%+ service-connected disabled veterans
College grant program for dependents of Iowa National Guard members killed in service
Veteran employment preference: absolute for disabled veterans
property_tax_notes: Military Homestead Tax Credit available for eligible veterans — apply with county assessor.
notes: Iowa's full military retirement pay exemption was phased in through 2024 — one of the more recent states to achieve full exemption.

name: Kansas
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Kansas state income tax
Active duty pay: 100% exempt while stationed or serving outside Kansas
Property tax relief: disabled veterans homestead exemption program
Free hunting and fishing license: resident veterans
Veteran preference in state employment
Kansas Veterans Home: long-term care for eligible veterans
State veterans cemeteries: free burial for eligible Kansas veterans
property_tax_notes: Homestead property tax exemption for disabled veterans — amounts vary. Contact Kansas Department of Revenue.
notes: Kansas's full military retirement exemption, combined with relatively lower property taxes and cost of living, makes it a solid retirement destination.

name: Kentucky
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Kentucky income tax
Active duty pay: 100% exempt while on active duty orders
Property tax exemption: up to $40,500 assessed value for 100% disabled veterans
Surviving spouse: property tax exemption transfers to surviving spouse
Free hunting and fishing license: resident veterans with service-connected disabilities
Service medal license plate options
Kentucky Veterans Center system: residential care for qualifying veterans
property_tax_notes: $40,500 assessed value exemption for 100% disabled veterans. At a typical Kentucky tax rate of ~1%, this saves ~$400/year in property taxes.
notes: Kentucky's strong retirement pay exemption makes it attractive for military retirees. Bluegrass region has lower cost of living than most coastal states.

name: Louisiana
stars: Full property tax exemption for 100% P&T disabled
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Louisiana state income tax
Active duty pay: Louisiana residents on active duty pay partially exempt
100% P&T disabled veteran: full property tax exemption on primary residence
Surviving spouse of 100% P&T veteran: full property tax exemption transfers
Free hunting and fishing license: 100% service-connected disabled veterans
Free admission to Louisiana state museums
Veteran employment preference in state hiring
property_tax_notes: Louisiana provides full property tax exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans — one of the strongest state property benefits in the South.
notes: Louisiana's 100% retirement exemption plus full property tax for P&T is a strong combination, despite higher state income tax rates for other income.

name: Maine
stars: Paraplegic veteran: full property tax exemption
highlights: Active duty military pay: 100% exempt from Maine income tax while on active duty outside ME
Military retirement pay: up to $10,000 exempt from Maine income tax
Property tax exemption: $6,000 assessed value for qualifying veterans (wartime service)
Paraplegic veteran: full property tax exemption on primary residence
Free hunting and fishing license: 100% service-connected disabled veterans
Maine Veterans' Homes: six facilities offering assisted living and skilled nursing care
Veteran preference in state employment
property_tax_notes: Paraplegic veterans receive full property tax exemption. Standard veteran exemption is $6,000 of assessed value.
notes: Maine's veterans' home system (6 facilities) is one of the most extensive in New England and provides high-quality care.

name: Maryland
stars: Purple Heart: free tuition at Maryland public colleges
highlights: Military retirement pay: up to $15,000 exempt from Maryland income tax (age 55+; $5,000 under 55)
Active duty combat pay: 100% exempt from Maryland income tax
100% disabled veteran: full property tax exemption on primary residence
Surviving spouse maintains exemption
Purple Heart recipient: free tuition at Maryland public colleges and universities
VetCash bonus payments for combat veterans who served in qualifying periods
Maryland Overseas Combat Pay: state income tax exemption for combat pay
property_tax_notes: Full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans on primary residence. Means-tested programs available.
notes: Maryland's free tuition for Purple Heart recipients is one of the more unique and meaningful state education benefits.

name: Massachusetts
stars: Active duty pay: 100% exempt from MA income tax
Free tuition for children of KIA veterans
highlights: Active duty military pay: 100% exempt from Massachusetts income tax
Veterans annuity: partial exemption from state income tax
Property tax exemption: $400–$1,500 reduction depending on service era and disability
Blind veterans: enhanced property tax exemption
Children of veterans killed in service or 100% disabled: free tuition at Massachusetts public colleges
Massachusetts National Guard: tuition waiver at state colleges
Strong VSO network: DAV, VFW, American Legion chapters statewide
property_tax_notes: Property tax exemptions range from $400 to $1,500+ depending on disability rating and service period.
notes: The free tuition for children of KIA veterans is one of the most meaningful state benefits. Massachusetts also has robust VSO infrastructure for claims assistance.

name: Michigan
stars: 100% property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt for those who entered service before 1997; partial exemption for post-1997
Property tax exemption: 100% for 100% disabled and unemployable veterans
Surviving spouse: maintains full property tax exemption
Free hunting and fishing license: 100% disabled veterans
Michigan Veterans Trust Fund: emergency financial assistance
Michigan Veterans Affairs Agency: VSO and claims support
Michigan Veterans' Homes: Grand Rapids and Marquette facilities
property_tax_notes: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% disabled AND unemployable veterans — one of the stronger property benefits in the Midwest.
notes: Michigan's 100% property tax exemption is for veterans who are both 100% disabled AND unemployable (TDIU). Verify specific criteria.

name: Minnesota
stars: Up to $300,000 home market value excluded (100% disabled)
highlights: Veterans Property Tax Market Value Exclusion: up to $300,000 home market value excluded for 100% disabled
Lower disability ratings: partial market value exclusions available
Surviving spouse: exclusion maintained after veteran's death
Military retirement pay: partially exempt from Minnesota income tax
Free state park vehicle permit: Purple Heart recipients and POW/MIA veterans
Minnesota GI Bill: education benefits for post-9/11 veterans at MN schools
Strong veteran employment preference for state government positions
property_tax_notes: The market value exclusion of up to $300,000 for 100% disabled veterans is one of the strongest property tax benefits in the Midwest — can eliminate property taxes entirely for many veterans.
notes: Minnesota's property tax exclusion is based on market value (not assessed value), making it particularly powerful in markets with high home values.

name: Mississippi
stars: 100% disabled: full ad valorem tax exemption
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Mississippi state income tax
Active duty pay: 100% exempt from Mississippi income tax
Ad valorem (property) tax exemption: 100% on primary residence for 100% disabled veterans
Disabled veteran homestead exemption
Free hunting and fishing license: 100% service-connected disabled veterans
Vehicle registration savings for disabled veterans
Mississippi Veterans Home Purchase Board: assistance with home financing
property_tax_notes: Full ad valorem (property) tax exemption on primary residence for 100% disabled veterans.
notes: Mississippi's combination of full retirement exemption and full property tax exemption for 100% disabled makes it one of the better Southern states for veteran finances.

name: Missouri
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Missouri state income tax
Active duty pay: 100% exempt while on active duty
Property tax credit: available for senior/disabled veterans (income-based)
Missouri Veterans Commission: 7 veterans homes providing residential care
Free hunting and fishing license: 100% service-connected disabled veterans
Veteran hiring preference in state government
Missouri Veterans Commission Emergency Assistance Fund
property_tax_notes: Property tax credit program for seniors and disabled veterans — income-based. Contact Missouri Department of Revenue.
notes: Missouri has a well-regarded veterans home system (7 facilities statewide) and strong VSO infrastructure.

name: Montana
highlights: Military retirement pay: up to $4,370/year exempt from Montana income tax
Active duty pay: partially exempt from Montana income tax while on active duty
Property tax exemption: $2,000–$6,000+ assessed value reduction for disabled veterans
Free hunting and fishing license: resident veterans with service-connected disabilities
Montana Veterans' Home in Columbia Falls: residential care for qualifying veterans
Veteran preference in state employment
State veterans cemeteries
property_tax_notes: Property tax exemption for disabled veterans varies by disability percentage. Contact Montana Department of Revenue.
notes: Montana has excellent hunting and fishing — the free licenses for disabled veterans are particularly valued in this state.

name: Nebraska
highlights: Military retirement pay: growing toward 100% exemption (phased — verify current year amount)
Homestead exemption: 100% disabled veterans receive full property tax exemption
Disabled veteran absolute hiring preference: 30%+ service-connected disabled
Free hunting and fishing license: active duty and deployed Guard/Reserve
Nebraska Veterans Aid Fund: emergency financial assistance for Nebraska veterans
Veteran hiring preference in state employment
State veterans cemeteries: Nebraska Veterans Cemetery
property_tax_notes: 100% disabled veterans receive full homestead property tax exemption on primary residence.
notes: Nebraska's retirement pay exemption is growing annually — check current legislation for the exact current-year exemption amount.

name: Nevada
highlights: NO state income tax on any income (military pay, retirement, wages)
Veteran property tax exemption: $2,000 assessed value per year
Disabled veteran: increased property tax exemption (up to $20,000 for high disability ratings)
Free veteran license plate options
Nevada Department of Veterans Services: statewide VSO and benefits support
Veteran hiring preference for state employment
Strong VA healthcare network in Las Vegas and Reno areas
property_tax_notes: Basic veteran exemption of $2,000 assessed value; up to $20,000 for high disability ratings.
notes: Nevada's no-income-tax status is the primary tax benefit. Property tax exemptions are modest but consistent.

name: New Hampshire
highlights: NO state income tax on wages, salaries, or military pay
Military pensions: not taxed (no state income tax on earned income)
Property tax credit: $700/year for qualifying veterans
100% disabled veteran: up to $4,000 additional property tax credit
Free hunting and fishing license: active duty and certain veterans
New Hampshire State Veterans Cemetery: free burial
Strong VSO network for VA claims support
property_tax_notes: $700/year property tax credit for qualifying veterans. Enhanced credit for 100% disabled veterans.
notes: New Hampshire's no-income-tax on wages plus the $700+ property tax credit makes it one of New England's most veteran-friendly states financially.

name: New Jersey
stars: 100% disabled veteran: full property tax exemption
Military pay exempt on active duty
highlights: Military pay: 100% exempt from New Jersey income tax while on active duty orders
Veterans property tax deduction: $250/year off property tax bill
100% disabled veteran: full property tax exemption on primary residence
Surviving spouse of 100% disabled veteran: maintains full exemption
State parks: free admission for disabled veterans
New Jersey Veterans Bonus: combat zone service bonus payments
Strong veteran hiring preference in state and local government
property_tax_notes: Full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans on primary residence — enormously valuable given New Jersey's high property tax rates (among highest in U.S.).
notes: Given New Jersey's very high property taxes, the full exemption for 100% disabled veterans can be worth $8,000–$20,000+/year in savings.

name: New Mexico
stars: 100% military retirement pay now fully exempt (2022)
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from New Mexico income tax (as of 2022)
Combat pay: 100% exempt from New Mexico income tax
Property tax exemption: $4,000 for veterans (basic); significantly more for 100% disabled
100% disabled veteran: full property tax exemption on primary residence
Free hunting and fishing license: 100% service-connected disabled veterans
New Mexico veterans bonus programs for qualifying combat veterans
Veteran preference: strong state employment preference
property_tax_notes: 100% disabled veterans receive full property tax exemption. Significant improvement with the 2022 legislative change making military retirement fully exempt.
notes: New Mexico's 2022 legislation making military retirement fully exempt was a major change — previously retirement was taxed. This significantly improved the state's veteran-friendliness.

name: New York
stars: Military pay: 100% exempt from NY state and city income tax
highlights: Military pay: 100% exempt from New York state AND New York City income tax while on active duty
Military pension: partially exempt for 20-year+ retirees (Alternative Veterans Exemption)
Veterans Alternative Exemption: property tax reduction on primary residence
Gold Star family: property tax exemption
Strong New York City and state veteran hiring preference
New York Veterans Income Tax Exemption for certain pension amounts
SUNY/CUNY tuition benefits for qualifying veterans
property_tax_notes: Alternative Veterans Exemption reduces assessed value for property tax purposes. Amounts vary by municipality — some NYC boroughs offer substantial reductions.
notes: New York's active duty pay exemption from both state AND city income tax is a significant benefit for NYC-area military — NYC's income tax alone is 3%+.

name: North Carolina
stars: Military retirement pay: substantial state exemption
highlights: Military retirement pay: up to $4,000 exempt; additional deduction based on years of service (Bailey v. NC settlement benefits)
Combat pay: 100% exempt from North Carolina income tax
National Guard and Reserve: free in-state tuition at NC public colleges for qualifying members
Free hunting and fishing license: resident veterans with service-connected disabilities
Veteran preference: strong state hiring preference
NC State Veterans Home system: multiple facilities
Property tax exclusion: disabled veterans homestead exclusion
property_tax_notes: Disabled veteran homestead exclusion available. Contact NC Department of Revenue for current amounts.
notes: North Carolina's Bailey settlement tax exemptions for retirees who retired before August 12, 1989 (and certain after) remain complex — consult a tax professional for military retirees.

name: North Dakota
stars: Military retirement: 100% exempt from ND income tax
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from North Dakota state income tax
Active duty pay: 100% exempt from North Dakota income tax while on active duty
Property tax credit: disabled veterans qualify for credit on primary residence
Veterans Land Board Loan Program: low-interest home and farm loans
Free hunting and fishing license: resident veterans with service-connected disabilities
Veteran hiring preference in state employment
North Dakota Veterans Home: residential care in Lisbon, ND
property_tax_notes: Property tax credit program for disabled veterans. North Dakota has relatively low property taxes overall.
notes: North Dakota's full military retirement exemption is strong. The state has relatively low cost of living and property taxes compared to coastal states.

name: Ohio
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Ohio state income tax
Active duty pay: 100% exempt from Ohio income tax
Disabled veteran homestead exemption: $50,000 property value reduction
100% disabled or killed in action: UNLIMITED homestead property tax exemption
Surviving spouse of 100% disabled veteran: maintains unlimited exemption
Free hunting and fishing license: 100% service-connected disabled veterans
Ohio Veterans Bonus: bonus payments for qualifying combat veterans
property_tax_notes: The UNLIMITED homestead exemption for 100% disabled or KIA veterans' families is one of the strongest property tax benefits in the entire country.
notes: Ohio's unlimited property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans is exceptional — effectively eliminates property taxes entirely on the primary residence.

name: Oklahoma
stars: 100% disabled veteran: full property tax exemption on residence
highlights: Military retirement pay: 75% exempt from Oklahoma income tax (up to $10,000 maximum)
Active duty pay: 100% exempt from Oklahoma income tax while on active duty
100% service-connected disabled veteran: full property tax exemption on primary residence
Disabled veteran: tiered property tax exemptions for ratings below 100%
Free hunting and fishing license: 60%+ service-connected disabled veterans
Oklahoma Veterans Education Assistance Grant (OVEAG): additional education benefits
Strong veteran hiring preference in state employment
property_tax_notes: Full property tax exemption for 100% service-connected disabled veterans on primary residence.
notes: Oklahoma's retirement pay exemption has a cap of $10,000, which means high-rank retirees may not get full benefit. Property exemption for 100% disabled is a standout benefit.

name: Oregon
stars: Oregon Veterans Home Loans (ODVA) — among the best in the country
highlights: Military retirement pay: up to $6,250/person exempt from Oregon income tax ($12,500 joint)
Active duty pay: exempt while serving outside Oregon
Property tax exemption: $22,500–$85,000 assessed value depending on disability rating and service era
Surviving spouse: maintains property tax exemption
Oregon Department of Veterans Affairs (ODVA) Home Loans: among the lowest fixed mortgage rates available to veterans
Free hunting and fishing license: 40%+ service-connected disabled veterans
Oregon Veterans Home: skilled nursing care for qualifying veterans
property_tax_notes: Property tax exemption ranges from $22,500 (basic) to $85,000+ assessed value depending on disability rating and service era.
notes: The ODVA home loan program is frequently cited as one of the best state veteran home loan programs in the country — competitive fixed rates with streamlined processing.

name: Pennsylvania
stars: Military pay + retirement: 100% exempt from PA income tax
highlights: Military pay: 100% exempt from Pennsylvania state income tax
Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Pennsylvania income tax (including military pensions)
Disabled veteran property tax relief through the Real Estate Tax Exemption Act
Blind veterans: enhanced property tax exemption
PA Soldiers and Sailors Home system: multiple residential care facilities
Strong veteran hiring preference
Pennsylvania Veterans Emergency Assistance Program
property_tax_notes: Disabled veterans may qualify for Real Estate Tax Exemption on primary residence — 100% disabled may receive full exemption.
notes: Pennsylvania's full exemption on both military pay AND retirement pay from state income tax is one of the clearest and most comprehensive income tax benefits of any mid-Atlantic state.

name: Rhode Island
stars: Military retirement pay: $15,000 exempt from RI income tax
highlights: Military retirement pay: up to $15,000 exempt from Rhode Island income tax
Combat pay: 100% exempt from Rhode Island income tax
Property tax exemption: $1,000 assessed value reduction for qualifying veterans
100% disabled veteran: up to $6,000 additional property tax exemption
Veterans Bonus: state combat service bonus payments
Rhode Island Veterans Home in Bristol: residential care facility
Veteran preference in state employment
property_tax_notes: Property tax exemption modest by national standards but consistent.
notes: Rhode Island is a small state but has a meaningful network of VSOs and veteran services relative to its population.

name: South Carolina
stars: 100% military retirement pay tax exempt (full phase-in complete)
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from South Carolina income tax (full phase-in completed 2022)
Combat pay: 100% exempt from South Carolina income tax
Disabled veteran property tax waiver: qualifying disabled veterans may receive full exemption on primary residence
Free vehicle license plates for veterans
Veteran preference: strong preference in state government hiring
South Carolina Veterans Home system: multiple facilities
State veterans cemeteries
property_tax_notes: Qualifying disabled veterans may receive full property tax waiver on primary residence — verify current eligibility requirements with county assessor.
notes: South Carolina's full retirement exemption was phased in over multiple years and reached 100% in 2022 — now one of the more veteran-friendly Southern states for retirees.

name: South Dakota
highlights: NO state income tax on any income (wages, retirement, military pay, investment income)
NO state inheritance tax or estate tax
Property tax freeze for disabled veterans (income-based)
Free veteran designation on driver's license
Free South Dakota state park access for veterans
Strong veteran employment preference in state government
South Dakota State Veterans Cemetery and veteran service organization network
property_tax_notes: Property tax freeze available for low-income disabled veterans.
notes: South Dakota is often cited as the #1 state for military retirees from a pure tax perspective — no income tax, no estate tax, relatively low property taxes, and low cost of living.

name: Tennessee
highlights: NO state income tax on wages, salaries, or military pay
Military retirement pay: not subject to Tennessee income tax (no income tax on wages/retirement)
Property tax relief: disabled veterans homestead exemption
100% disabled veteran: full property tax exemption on primary residence in some counties
Free hunting and fishing license: disabled veterans with qualifying ratings
Veteran preference in state employment
Tennessee State Veterans Homes: multiple facilities
property_tax_notes: Tennessee eliminated its Hall Income Tax (on investment income) in 2021 — the state now has zero income tax. Property tax exemptions for disabled veterans vary by county.
notes: Tennessee joined the no-income-tax states fully in 2021 with the elimination of the Hall Income Tax. Combined with low cost of living (outside Nashville), it's a growing retirement destination.

name: Texas
stars: 100% disabled: full property tax exemption statewide
highlights: NO state income tax on any income (wages, military pay, retirement pay, investment income)
100% service-connected disabled veteran: FULL property tax exemption on primary residence (statewide law)
Surviving spouse of 100% disabled veteran: full exemption maintained
70–99% disabled veteran: significant property tax reductions (tiered)
Free hunting and fishing license for veterans with 50%+ service-connected disability
Veterans Land Board: home, land, and home improvement loans at below-market rates
Texas Hazlewood Act: tuition waiver at Texas public colleges for veterans, their spouses, and children
Strong veteran hiring preference in state and local government
property_tax_notes: Texas's full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans is statewide law (not optional by county) — at a typical Texas effective tax rate of 1.6%, a $400,000 home saves $6,400/year in property taxes.
notes: Texas is consistently ranked #1 or #2 in veteran-friendliness. The combination of no income tax, full property tax exemption for 100% disabled, the Hazlewood tuition waiver for dependents, and the VLB loan program is unmatched by any other state.

name: Utah
stars: Active duty pay: 100% exempt from UT income tax
highlights: Active duty military pay: 100% exempt from Utah income tax
Military retirement pay: up to $1,000 tax credit (not deduction — direct reduction in taxes owed)
Property tax abatement: qualifying veteran homeowners (income-based)
Free hunting and fishing license: active duty deployed and certain disabled veterans
Utah Veteran Employment Initiative: strong job placement programs
Veteran preference: state employment preference for qualifying veterans
Utah State Veterans Nursing Home: Payson facility
property_tax_notes: Property tax abatement available for qualifying veterans — income-based. Contact Utah State Tax Commission.
notes: Utah's $1,000 military retirement tax credit is a direct credit (not a deduction), making it more valuable dollar-for-dollar than exemptions on small amounts of income.

name: Vermont
stars: Military pay: 100% exempt from VT income tax
highlights: Active duty military pay: 100% exempt from Vermont income tax
Military retirement pay: up to $10,000 exempt from Vermont income tax
Property tax credit: income-based credit for veterans with low-to-moderate income
Disabled veteran: additional property tax benefits
Vermont Veterans Home in Bennington: residential care
Veteran preference in state employment
Free veteran ID cards
property_tax_notes: Income-based property tax credit available for qualifying veterans.
notes: Vermont's veteran benefits are modest by comparison but the active duty pay exemption provides meaningful relief for stationed servicemembers.

name: Virginia
stars: 100% P&T disabled: full property tax exemption statewide
highlights: 100% P&T disabled veteran: FULL property tax exemption on primary residence (statewide law as of 2011)
Surviving spouse of 100% P&T disabled veteran: full exemption maintained
Military retirement pay: partially taxable in Virginia (no full exemption — notable disadvantage vs. neighboring states)
Virginia Military Survivors & Dependents Education Program (VMSDEP): free tuition at Virginia public colleges for dependents of 100% disabled or KIA veterans
Virginia National Guard: tuition assistance supplements
Veteran license plate options
Strong veteran employment preference in state government
property_tax_notes: Full property tax exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans is statewide and automatic — no annual re-application required. On Northern Virginia home values ($600K–$1M+), this saves $7,000–$12,000+/year.
notes: Virginia has a significant asymmetry: full property tax exemption for 100% P&T is outstanding, but military retirement pay IS taxable unlike most Southern and Mid-Atlantic peer states. Consider this when making retirement location decisions.

name: Washington
highlights: NO state income tax on any income (wages, military pay, retirement pay)
Military retirement pay: not subject to Washington state income tax (no state income tax)
Free hunting and fishing license: active duty servicemembers and veterans with qualifying disabilities
Property tax exemption: income-based program for veterans with disabilities
Free burial: Washington State Veterans Cemetery
Washington State Department of Veterans Affairs: comprehensive VSO network
College fee waiver: National Guard members at Washington state public institutions
property_tax_notes: Income-based property tax exemption for disabled veterans. Washington does have a capital gains tax (7%) on gains above $250,000 — relevant for high-asset military investors.
notes: Washington State enacted a capital gains tax in 2023 (7% on gains above $250K) — the primary exception to the 'no income tax' characterization. Military pay and retirement remain untaxed.

name: West Virginia
stars: Full property tax exemption for 100% disabled
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from West Virginia state income tax
Active duty pay: 100% exempt from West Virginia income tax
Property tax exemption: $20,000 assessed value reduction for qualifying veterans
100% totally and permanently disabled veteran: full property tax exemption on primary residence
Free hunting and fishing license: resident veterans with service-connected disabilities
West Virginia State Veterans Cemetery
Veteran preference in state government employment
property_tax_notes: Full property tax exemption for 100% totally and permanently disabled veterans on primary residence.
notes: West Virginia has very low property tax rates overall (among the lowest in the country) — the exemptions apply to an already modest tax burden.

name: Wisconsin
stars: Full property tax exemption for 100% disabled
highlights: Military retirement pay: 100% exempt from Wisconsin state income tax (phased in; fully exempt since 2017)
Active duty pay: 100% exempt from Wisconsin income tax
100% disabled veteran: full property tax exemption on primary residence
Surviving spouse: maintains full property tax exemption
Free hunting and fishing license: 100% service-connected disabled veterans
Wisconsin Veterans Home system: three facilities (King, Union Grove, Chippewa Falls)
Wisconsin Veterans Housing Loan Guarantee: low-interest home loans
property_tax_notes: Full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans on primary residence — strong benefit in Wisconsin's property-tax-heavy environment.
notes: Wisconsin's Veterans Home system (3 locations) is one of the largest in the Midwest and well-regarded for care quality.

name: Wyoming
highlights: NO state income tax on any income (wages, military pay, retirement pay)
Property tax exemption: up to $3,000 assessed value for qualifying veterans
Disabled veteran: additional property tax exemption based on disability rating
Hunting and fishing priority access for veterans: early license purchasing windows
Free admission to Wyoming state museums for veterans
Veteran preference in state government employment
Wyoming Veterans Commission: VSO support and benefits navigation
property_tax_notes: Base veteran property tax exemption of $3,000 assessed value. Wyoming has among the lowest property tax effective rates in the country.
notes: Wyoming is one of only 9 states with no income tax. Combined with very low property tax rates and a low cost of living, it is consistently rated among the most financially favorable states for veterans.
